title: SharePoint Online Site and Web Creation Activity
spl:
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload=SharePoint earliest=-7d
| where match(Operation, "(?i)site.*creat|new.*site|create.*web")
| eval actor=coalesce(UserId, UserPrincipalName, userId, "")
| eval site=coalesce(SiteUrl, ObjectId, "")
| stats count values(Operation) as ops by actor, site
| sort -count
description: New site or web creation events in SharePoint Online highlight shadow IT, partner onboarding, or attacker persistence through new collaboration surfaces.
value: Lets governance teams reconcile sprawl against approved site provisioning workflows and gives security teams early visibility when creation spikes follow account compromise.
implementation: Enable SharePoint workload in the Office 365 Management Activity input. `Operation` strings evolve—maintain a lookup of approved operations or site templates. Exclude known automation service principals via `UserId`. Forward notable clusters to GRC for records management.
visualization: Table (actor × site), timechart of creations, single value (distinct creators).
knownFalsePositives: New project sites, hub creation during migrations, and pilot teams spinning up test sites.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
grandmaExplanation: We watch SharePoint sharing and file pulls, so odd permission changes or big downloads are visible before they spread.
